ogTitle: Worlds End vs Woronora Dam: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Worlds End and Woronora Dam.
heading: Worlds End vs Woronora Dam — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Worlds End, New South Wales and Woronora Dam, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Worlds End has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Woronora Dam (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Worlds End or Woronora Dam safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Worlds End has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Woronora Dam scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Worlds End scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Woronora Dam scores 57/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Worlds End or Woronora Dam?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Worlds End has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.5% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
